Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to analyze an event log against two parts normative strict rules and desiderata recommended conditions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Part 1 Normative Process Behavior Strict Rules Sequence required 1 Application Received 2 Credit Check before Risk Assessment 3 Risk Assessment before Loan Decision 4 Loan Decision before Inform Applicant 5 Inform Applicant after Loan Decision 6 If Approved Finalize Documentation then Close Case 7 If Rejected Close Case without Finalize Documentation Also no skipping no reversing order no contradictory actions

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me break down the analysis case by case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Check sequence App Rec Credit Check Credit Check Risk Assessment 0930 before 1000 Risk Assessment Loan Decision Loan Decision Inform Applicant 1100 before 1300 Approved Finalize Doc Close Case 1330 before 1400 No violations Perfect compliance with normative rules

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 Check violations 1 Risk Assessment at 0820 occurs before Credit Check at 0900 This violates Credit Check must occur before Risk Assessment 2 Also there is a skip reversal Credit Check should be before Risk Assessment but here Risk Assessment is done first then Credit Check 3 Loan Decision is Rejected 0930 4 Finalize Documentation at 1000 occurs after rejection This violates If loan is rejected do not finalize documentation before closing the case and Performing Finalize Documentation after a rejection is not allowed 5 Close Case at 1030 happens after Finalize Documentation but since it was rejected documentation should not have been finalized

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 complies with all normative strict rules The delays are desideratum issues only

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 desiderata Credit Check within 24h Yes Inform within 48h Yes Follow up Call Not present So Case 1 missing follow up call only

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait in Case 2 there is NO Inform Applicant activity listed This is actually a normative violation too Following the loan decision the applicant must be informed of the outcome In Case 2 Inform Applicant is missing entirely

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So Case 2 also violates the normative rule that Inform Applicant must occur after Loan Decision skipping it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 normative violations 1 Risk Assessment before Credit Check wrong order 2 Credit Check after Risk Assessment reversed order relative to required sequence 3 Skipping Inform Applicant required step missing after Loan Decision 4 Finalizing Documentation after Rejection 5 Closing case without informing applicant

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 desiderata Credit Check at Jan 14 1000 Application at Jan 12 0900 Difference more than 24 hours so violates the 24 hour recommendation Loan Decision Jan 14 1200 Inform Applicant Jan 16 1500 Difference about 51 hours so violates the 48 hour recommendation No Follow up Call listed So Case 3 fails all three desiderata conditions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 for desiderata Credit Check is within 24h so that timing recommendation is technically satisfied The applicant was never informed so the prompt notification desideratum is failed No follow up call performed

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So all cases fail at least the follow up call Only Case 1 meets the timing ones Case 3 fails both timing ones Case 2 fails notification and has normative issues

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Case 1 For the missing follow up call justification could be that the case was straightforward approved quickly documentation finalized same day and closed within hours so there was little need for alignment check or the process was fully automated

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Case 2 Maybe an experienced risk officer made a preliminary evaluation based on initial documents while waiting for the formal credit check results Or there was a system error logging error where Risk Assessment was recorded early but actually done later Or urgency led to parallel processing Missing Inform Applicant could be an administrative oversight or the rejection was communicated through another channel not logged Finalize Documentation after Rejection could be a clerical error or confusion about the decision status

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Case 3 Credit Check delayed more than 24h Maybe the applicant submitted incomplete initial documents or there was a queue backlog or the credit bureau was slow The notification delay could result from internal review protocols or difficulty reaching the applicant No Follow up Call maybe the staff considered the case straightforward or resource constraints

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 No normative violations Sequence is perfect App Credit Risk Decision Approved Inform Finalize Close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Multiple violations Wrong order Risk Assessment performed before Credit Check Skipped step Inform Applicant is absent Contradictory action Finalize Documentation performed after Rejection Improper closure logic Case closed after erroneous documentation finalization without informing applicant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 No normative violations Sequence correct App Credit Risk Decision Approved Inform Finalize Close Even though delayed the strict ordering is preserved and the approved path is followed correctly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 fails only the follow up call desideratum Case 2 fails notification promptness and follow up call while credit check timing is technically within 24h Case 3 fails delayed credit check delayed notification and missing follow up call
